attrs.behavior: followed aircraft maneuvers, positioned to rear at ~2000m

An unidentified craft approximately 250 m in length was tracked on radar for 50 seconds crossing the trajectory of flight AF 3532, disappearing simultaneously from crew visual observation and radar scopes; CODA investigation ruled out a weather balloon.
Unknown aerial objects tracked by radar at USAF and RAF bases Lakenheath and Bentwaters during the night of August 13–14, 1956, were judged 'unidentified' in the 1969 Condon Commission report.

Radar at Bentwaters detected an unidentified object moving east to west at 2000-4000 mph at 2255 hours; corroborated by visual sightings from Bentwaters control tower personnel and a military transport pilot who saw a bright light pass beneath his aircraft at approximately 1200 m altitude.

During the night of September 18–19, 1976, a UAP incident over Tehran was documented in a DIA report obtained via the Freedom of Information Act, corroborated by interviews with Iranian generals and an air traffic controller.

Flight AR 674 and a standby aircraft observed a luminous object with three lights (one red) near San Carlos airport; the object caused airport lights to fail, maneuvered behind and in front of the aircraft, ascended vertically, and departed toward the Andes Cordillera, witnessed by crew, passengers, a second aircraft, airport controllers, and local inhabitants.

incidents.summary: Aerolineas Argentinas flight AR 674 on approach to San Carlos de Bariloche observed an unidentified object resembling a large aircraft fly parallel to it on its right side, coinciding with two separate power outages that extinguished the town and airport lights.

An aerial phenomenon near Antananarivo, Madagascar was estimated to be traveling at approximately 3000 km/h; General Fleurquin assembled a scientific commission to investigate, and the sighting was later described in GEPA bulletin no. 6 (2nd half of 1964).

Former Air Force Lieutenant-Colonel Jean-Pierre Fartek, a Mirage III pilot stationed at Dijon, reported a sighting of a saucer close to the ground on December 9, 1979, which was subsequently investigated by GEPAN/SEPRA.

Military personnel at a Russian army missile base near Kapustin Yar submitted written depositions of visual UFO sightings made on the night of July 28-29, 1989, as documented in KGB-declassified files.

entities.name: unidentified object on six legs with central pivot

attrs.description: shape reminiscent of Dauphine automobile, resting on six legs with a central pivot

incidents.summary: Lavender farmer Maurice Masse encountered an unidentified object resting in his field and two small beings, one of whom immobilized him with a tube-like device, investigated in-depth by the Gendarmerie Nationale.

Second lieutenant Volochine and soldier Tichaev, later joined by a guard team, observed a soundless disc-shaped object with a phosphorescent green light hovering 20 m above a missile depot and illuminating it with a moving beam of light for approximately two hours.

Gendarmerie investigation at Valensole confirmed physical ground traces including a depression, a cylindrical hole 18 cm in diameter and 40 cm deep, and dried lavender beds along the object's flight axis, corroborating witness Maurice Masse's account.

An unidentified craft flew over Antananarivo at 50–100 meters altitude, causing shop lights to extinguish and animals to exhibit extreme anxiety, including panic among zebus in the local park, before departing in a westerly direction.

An identical craft was observed 150 km from Antananarivo above a farm school two to three minutes after the Antananarivo sighting, again causing herd panic; if the same craft, its speed would have been approximately 3000 km/h.
incidents.location: farm school, 150 km from Antananarivo, Madagascar

incidents.summary: Two young children watching a family herd on the high plateaus of France reported a UFO incident near Cussac, Cantal, investigated by GEPAN/SEPRA and later re-examined in 1978.

attrs.behavior: jerky movements, abrupt starts and stops, periods of immobility, supersonic escape speed

Second Lieutenant Volochine sighted a disk 4-5 m in diameter surmounted by a brightly lit hemispherical dome at a distance ranging from several kilometers down to 300 m, from 2330 to 0130 hours.

Two child witnesses at an unspecified site reported a sighting accompanied by a hissing sound, strong sulfur odor, and physiological disorders including running eyes lasting several days; a judicial inquiry concluded the witnesses were sincere and the testimony consistent.

attrs.behavior: spiral movement at high speed

Unidentified flying objects observed flying in formation, breaking off and reforming in trail formation, disappearing and reappearing periodically, visible to the naked eye when reflecting sunlight.
Personnel from Medford Airways sighted objects with distinguishable wings and notified Medford Tower and Medford Radio; both facilities observed the objects using theodalite and binoculars.

One white tubular object observed at 1200 hours, approximately 6 miles in front and over aircraft.
1st Lt Kirshbaum reported a stationary object with a glint of light on canopy directly in the flight path at Fort Devens, Massachusetts.
Radar operator aboard RB-29 test flight observed radar interference on scope during operation from RB-29 aircraft number 45-21824.
RB-29 aircraft from the 514th Reconnaissance Squadron sighted a white billowing substance at 30,000-35,000 ft during a weather reconnaissance mission.

Paul Herring documented the course of an atmospheric disturbance near Kodiak on April 8, 1949.
RB-29 aircraft radar detected interference consisting of pulses approximately 10 miles apart covering the Guam area, suspected to be intentional jamming.

Two unidentified objects were sighted off the port and starboard bows at specified bearings and altitudes during February 4-6, 1949.
